"Dear Call of Duty Team,

I'm writing to express my enthusiasm for the classic Warzone maps, Verdansk and Caldera. With the release of the new Warzone, I understand the focus has shifted, but I'd love to see these iconic maps return with the original graphics and mechanics as playable options.

Not all players have adapted to the new Warzone, and many, like myself, miss the nostalgic experience of Verdansk and Caldera. Bringing them back would:

1. Cater to loyal fans who prefer the classic maps.
2. Offer variety and choice for players.
3. Preserve the game's history and legacy.

Microsoft's acquisition brings opportunities for innovation while respecting the game's roots. I hope you'll consider reinstating Verdansk and Caldera as playable maps.

The narration suggests Mason being dead is canon, the footage suggests otherwise, so a smart move on their end to keep the Black Ops 2 campaign ambigous regarding which ending is canon

All Black ops games in order:
World at war (1942-45)
Black ops 1 (1961-68)
Black ops Cold War (1981-84)
Black ops 2 (1986-89 flashbacks)
Black ops 6 (1991)
Black ops 2 (main 2025 missions)
Black ops 4 (2043)
Black ops 3 (2065)

20k views? Not bad for a blizzard game

1. Lock AIM: Automatically focuses on the nearest target.

2. Directional Aim Assist: Adjusts the aim based on the player’s movement direction.

3. Sticky Targeting: Keeps the aim locked onto a target even when the player moves.

5. Shot Offset: Compensates for the shot's displacement relative to the aim.

6. Aim Assist Precision: Improves shot accuracy as the aim gets closer to a target.

7. DeadZone Line: Defines an area where the aim does not adjust to avoid minor corrections.

8. Aim Snap: Quickly adjusts the aim to align with a target upon getting close.

9. Aim Throttle: Adjusts aim sensitivity when approaching a target.

10. Bullet Magnetism: Increases the chance of hitting a target even if the shot is not perfectly aligned. ( and the Cronus improve this in the hack to pull the player to aim )

When will they learn that the league needs to be open to more countries and disable aim assist for High Elos, Top 250, and Streamers to ensure a fair and skill-based game?

Dear Call of Duty Team,

my thoughts on the graphics in your recent games. I've played Black Ops 6 and was surprised to find that the graphics didn't seem to have improved much compared to Modern Warfare 3. In fact, I still believe that Modern Warfare 2019 has the best graphics in the series so far.

I think this might be due to the fact that all Call of Duty games are now developed under the same umbrella at COD HQ, leading to a homogenization of the visual style and graphics quality. While consistency is important, I believe each game should have its own unique identity and push the boundaries of what's possible.

What's even more disappointing is that Black Ops 6 was in development for 4 years, which is plenty of time to make significant improvements to the game's visuals. I expect more from a game that's had such a long development cycle.

Unfortunately, it feels like every game is starting to feel similar, and the graphics are no exception. I hope you'll take my feedback into consideration and encourage more innovation and experimentation in the visual department.

Sincerely,
ZDGAMING

Dear Call of Duty Team,

I'm writing to bring to your attention an important issue regarding the upcoming Black Ops 6. As a dedicated player, I'm excited about the new game, but I'm also concerned about a problem that has affected previous titles: shared audio settings between multiplayer and Warzone.

Warzone, in particular, has struggled with subpar audio quality, making it difficult for players to fully immerse themselves in the game. The lack of clear and precise sound effects, such as footsteps and gunfire, has hindered the overall gaming experience.

I urge you to address this issue in Black Ops 6 by implementing separate audio settings or profiles for multiplayer and Warzone. This would allow your team to optimize audio for each game independently, ensuring that Black Ops 6 multiplayer and Warzone receive the attention they deserve.

By doing so, you'll not only enhance the audio experience in Black Ops 6 but also have the opportunity to revamp and improve Warzone's audio, providing a better experience for players.

Please consider this request as you continue to develop Black Ops 6. I eagerly await the game's release and hope that this issue will be resolved.

Thank you for your time and attention.

Sincerely,

Zdgaming

Dear Call of Duty Team,

I'm writing to bring to your attention an important issue regarding the upcoming Black Ops 6. As a dedicated player, I'm excited about the new game, but I'm also concerned about a problem that has affected previous titles: shared audio settings between multiplayer and Warzone.

Warzone, in particular, has struggled with subpar audio quality, making it difficult for players to fully immerse themselves in the game. The lack of clear and precise sound effects, such as footsteps and gunfire, has hindered the overall gaming experience.

I urge you to address this issue in Black Ops 6 by implementing separate audio settings or profiles for multiplayer and Warzone. This would allow your team to optimize audio for each game independently, ensuring that Black Ops 6 multiplayer and Warzone receive the attention they deserve.

By doing so, you'll not only enhance the audio experience in Black Ops 6 but also have the opportunity to revamp and improve Warzone's audio, providing a better experience for players.

Please consider this request as you continue to develop Black Ops 6. I eagerly await the game's release and hope that this issue will be resolved.

Thank you for your time and attention.

Sincerely,

Zdgaming

Unfortunately if you are trying to survive sweaty sweats in the amped up SBMM or EOMM, your best bet is to find an operator skin that is as discreet as possible without flashy tracers that make it even more difficult to see your enemy while firing.   Love the blueprints as well, great artwork design!  That said, by the time I swap out the attachments to make the weapon META it no longer looks cool and you have to run META guns because again, the SBMM is cranked up so high you are dead unless you use META guns.  So really, the only thing left is the finishing moves which are definitely cool.  I just struggle spending that much money when all I can use out of the DLC is the finishing move.  Not trying to be negative, I love the artwork, but the assets are almost impossible to use without dying a lot more often.
